sourceMovie.title: All Things Fair
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: All Things Fair (1995) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Bo Widerberg's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Allied
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If All Things Fair clicked for you, Allied (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after All Things Fair?
faq.answer: Try Allied, Suite Française, Atonement—each shares DNA with All Things Fair while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is All Things Fair?
faq.answer: All Things Fair (1995) is best described as Drama, Romance, War. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is All Things Fair worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
